pages.raw_text:
Indications today are strong that
Fred DeGraff, printer, will succeed
to the council from the Third ward
to fill the vacancy caused by the
death of Harry G.. Stimmell. De
CRAFTS friends, as we'll as himself,
are working hard for it. DeGraff
heads the Bolo Civic club, 40
strong, and with this backing and
influences, his chances seem excep-
tionally good.


Ever since it was first rumored
that Harry G. Stimmell, council
man from the Third ward. would
resign while he was back East a
few months ago, Arthur B. Baines,
ex-councilman and Washington
Water Power Co. man, has been
negotiating for sufficient follow.
ing to secure the job. Some repub-
licans are inclined to favor him be-
cause he is a republican, while dem
ocrats want him because, while he
may be republican, when he was
councilman before he always voted
with the democrats, Just as n. s.
Pratt, democrat, now always sides
with the republican councilman.
